user_facing_requirements: Users can browse or filter by country.
Users can browse or filter by system/operator.
Users can browse or filter by meeting date.
Users can browse or filter by racecourse.
Users can see first-race time where officially confirmed.
Users can see all race times where officially confirmed.
Users can see pending status where official racecard/time data is not yet available.
Users can see coverage gaps where data needs manual review or source structure is unavailable.
Users can see Singapore as legacy/no active racing.
Users can see official annual source links.
Users can see official rolling/racecard source links where available.
Users can see stale_status or pending status.
Users can understand this is static/manual, not live coverage.
operations_requirements: Maintainers now have an ingestion/source plan layer.
Maintainers now have an annual candidate fixture layer.
Maintainers now have a rolling/racecard confirmation layer.
Maintainers now have a merged display timetable layer.
Maintainers now have a user-facing timetable route.
Maintainers now have source trace from display records back to annual and rolling data.
Maintainers now have explicit pending records.
Maintainers now have explicit coverage gaps.
Maintainers now have validation coverage for the full v0 chain.
Maintainers now have a clear next path toward parser/cron/source-specific automation.
non_goals: no claim of complete worldwide coverage
